benefits: Oncinotis glabrata has been traditionally used in African folk medicine, and research has identified its potential health benefits. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. It is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of infections. Studies have shown that the plant's extracts exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which could contribute to its therapeutic effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits and its applications in modern medicine.

intro: Oncinotis glabrata, commonly known as the African or Indian forest vine, is a perennial climbing plant native to tropical Africa and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Oncinotis glabrata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Oncinotis glabrata is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever and rheumatism.
